metadata.title: Zach Bush MD — soil, microbiome, regenerative agriculture · numbness has its own pain | Coherence Network
metadata.description: A welcome to Dr Zach Bush — internal-medicine, endocrinology, and hospice-care physician turned founder of Intrinsic Health, Seraphic Group, and the non-profit Farmer's Footprint. Soil health and the human microbiome as one continuous body. The teaching 'numbness has its own pain' is named here.

hero.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 30%, hsl(95 60% 45% / 0.55) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 80%, hsl(25 30% 25% / 0.7) 0%, transparent 60%), linear-gradient(180deg, hsl(100 40% 60%) 0%, hsl(45 30% 35%) 50%, hsl(25 35% 18%) 100%)
hero.eyebrow: Triple board · physician → soil-and-microbiome teacher · Intrinsic Health · Farmer's Footprint
hero.name: Zach Bush
hero.welcome_md:
Internal medicine, endocrinology, and hospice care — three board certifications and seventeen years inside academic oncology research and clinical practice before he walked away. What he walked toward: the recognition that chronic disease in the human body cannot be separated from the health of the soil under our feet, and that the microbiome connecting plant, soil, animal, and human is one continuous body that has been broken by industrial agriculture and can be reconnected by regenerative practice.

*The teaching this body has named most often: *numbness has its own pain*. Bush's framing, surfaced through Amanda Walsh quoting him on the Inspired Evolution podcast. Urs was physically in the field at one of his Emergence Conference talks; the contact was direct, not secondhand.*

facts.label: Born
facts.value_md: 1969, rural Virginia. Undergraduate and medical degree from the University of Colorado / CU School of Medicine.

facts.label: Triple board
facts.value_md: Internal medicine · endocrinology · hospice and palliative care. Seventeen years in academia, cancer-therapy research, and clinical practice before leaving institutional medicine.

facts.label: Founded
facts.value_md: **Seraphic Group** (parent company) · **Intrinsic Health** (immersive community + learning experience for intrinsic healing) · **Farmer's Footprint** (non-profit accelerating universal adoption of regenerative agriculture, founded 2018 within Project Biome)

facts.label: Field he names
facts.value_md: Soil-microbiome-human-microbiome continuity; regenerative agriculture as a change in farmer philosophy (stop killing weeds, start co-creating with biodiversity); the microbiome as the bridge between plant, soil, and human health.

note_from_body.body_md: Zach Bush's public archive is extensive — his own site, the Farmer's Footprint documentary series, dozens of podcast appearances. This page recognises the role his teaching plays in this lineage and the specific phrase *numbness has its own pain* that has travelled through it.

articles.body_md:
Bush's frame on his own pivot, repeated across interviews: he was a triple-board-certified academic physician running cancer-therapy research when the funding shifted from inside the institution and a promising line of work was cut. He walked away — not in protest, but in recognition that the question he cared about (what actually heals chronic disease) was not the question modern medicine was structured to answer. He opened a nutrition clinic, then began visiting farmers across the US, and the picture that assembled itself was that the microbiome under the soil and the microbiome inside the human gut are continuous; killing the first inevitably injures the second.

From that recognition, Farmer's Footprint emerged in 2018 — first as a documentary series, then as a movement organising the adoption of regenerative agriculture. The mission, in his framing: not soil conservation as a defence against further damage, but *regeneration* as the active practice of reconnecting plant to soil to animal to human.
articles.heading: The pivot from disease-management to regeneration

articles.body_md:
- *Numbness has its own pain* → named in this body via [Amanda Walsh](/people/amanda-walsh) quoting him; pairs with [lc-ground-harder-when-field-quickens](/vision/lc-ground-harder-when-field-quickens) (the body's practice of grounding harder when the field accelerates).
- The microbiome-as-continuous-body teaching → pairs with [lc-bioelectric-pattern](/vision/lc-bioelectric-pattern) and Michael Levin's pattern-precedes-substrate teaching at a different scale (soil ↔ gut ↔ tissue).
- Regenerative agriculture as a posture, not a technique → resonant with the body's practice of [lc-tending-over-producing](/vision/lc-tending-over-producing) and [lc-future-already-shaping](/vision/lc-future-already-shaping) (regenerate the form; the substance follows).
- Direct in-person encounter at an Emergence Conference (year not yet recorded in this trace); the contact entered this body as real presence, not as media.
articles.heading: What Zach Bush has given the Coherence Network
